name: PHP Version Manager (Tauri + Rust + React)
description: Cross-platform GUI to install, switch, and remove PHP runtimes—integrity-verified downloads, atomic filesystem operations with rollback, and a memory-safe Rust core behind the React UI.
longDescription: phpvm targets developers who juggle multiple PHP versions: a Tauri shell hosts a React UI that lists installed and available releases, tracks download cache with checksum verification, switches active versions with PATH updates, and removes unused builds. Rust on the backend provides memory-safe file operations with rollback support so partial installs are recoverable; the interface exposes progress reporting and settings for mirrors and cache locations. The design treats runtime binaries as supply-chain inputs that must be verified before execution.
features: Download and install official PHP builds with integrity checks
One-click activation with automatic PATH updates
Tabbed UI for installed, available, cache, and settings views
Cross-platform focus with primary support for Windows and Linux
highlights: Rust-backed filesystem mutations with rollback—reduces bricked dev shells from half-written trees
Operational focus: checksum-verified tarballs before activation
Developer-experience oriented tabbed workflows with explicit destructive-action UX
Separation between UI state and installation engine for easier auditing
challenges: Filesystem and PATH mutations are risky on end-user machines; mistakes brick shells or leave partial installs.
solutions: Atomic operations with rollback, verified checksum pipeline, and explicit UX around destructive actions.
security: Verify download checksums before install; prefer official mirrors
Avoid running the GUI with elevated privileges unless necessary
Store settings without embedding secrets or API tokens
Atomic install paths prevent inconsistent half-upgrades observable by other tools
User PATH mutations are explicit in UX to avoid silent system-wide changes without consent
